perturbation.original_text: (d) (I) Each Party is furnishing its Information hereunder in consideration of the other Party’s agreement for a period of eighteen (18) months that it and its affiliates (as defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) will not (and will not assist, provide or arrange financing to or for others or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, acting alone or in concert with others, unless specifically requested in writing in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body): (i) acquire or offer, seek or propose to acquire (or request permission to do so), ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Exchange Act) of all or substantially all of the assets or businesses of other Party or any securities, bank debt or trade debt issued by other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party) (other than purchases of up to 5% of such securities in connection with such Party’s ordinary cash management practices and without reference to or knowledge of such other Party’s Information), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or the policies of other Party or to obtain representation on the other Party’s Board of Directors, or solicit, or participate in the solicitation of, any proxies, consents or votes with respect to any securities of the other Party or with respect to any plan of reorganization filed by the other Party or any other person in connection with a bankruptcy or similar proceeding under state or federal law involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing or (iv) make any public announcement with respect to the foregoing. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement (including the foregoing), nothing in this Section 4(d) shall prohibit either Party from submitting a confidential proposal to the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body) with respect to any action described in this Section 4(d)(I), provided that such proposal is not of a type that would require the other Party to make a public disclosure thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) (I) Each Party is furnishing its Information hereunder in consideration of the other Party’s agreement for a period of eighteen (18) months that it and its affiliates (as defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) will not (and will not assist, provide or arrange financing to or for others or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, acting alone or in concert with others, unless specifically requested in writing in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body): (i) acquire or offer, seek or propose to acquire (or request permission to do so), ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Exchange Act) of all or substantially all of the assets or businesses of other Party or any securities, bank debt or trade debt issued by other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party) (other than purchases of up to 5% of such securities in connection with such Party’s ordinary cash management practices and without reference to or knowledge of such other Party’s Information), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or the policies of other Party or to obtain representation on the other Party’s Board of Directors, or solicit, or participate in the solicitation of, any proxies, consents or votes with respect to any securities of the other Party or with respect to any plan of reorganization filed by the other Party or any other person in connection with a bankruptcy or similar proceeding under state or federal law involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing or (iv) make any public announcement with respect to the foregoing. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement (including the foregoing), nothing in this Section 4(d) shall prohibit either Party from submitting a confidential proposal to the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body) with respect to any action described in this Section 4(d)(I), provided that such proposal is not of a type that would require the other Party to make a public disclosure thereof. This restriction will automatically be lifted if either party receives an unsolicited offer from a third party during this period.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'This restriction will automatically be lifted if either party receives an unsolicited offer from a third party during this period.' creates an in-text contradiction. While the paragraph initially imposes an eighteen-month restriction on actions like acquiring assets or influencing management, the added sentence now provides a condition for the termination of these restrictions (receipt of an unsolicited offer), potentially shortening the eighteen-month period and creating uncertainty about when the restrictions truly apply. This directly contradicts the initial time constraint.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) (I) Each Party is furnishing its Information hereunder in consideration of the other Party’s agreement for a period of eighteen (18) months that it and its affiliates (as defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) will not (and will not assist, provide or arrange financing to or for others or encourage others to), directly or indirectly, acting alone or in concert with others, unless specifically requested in writing in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body): (i) acquire or offer, seek or propose to acquire (or request permission to do so), ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Exchange Act) of all or substantially all of the assets or businesses of other Party or any securities, bank debt or trade debt issued by other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party) (other than purchases of up to 5% of such securities in connection with such Party’s ordinary cash management practices and without reference to or knowledge of such other Party’s Information), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control the management or the policies of other Party or to obtain representation on the other Party’s Board of Directors, or solicit, or participate in the solicitation of, any proxies, consents or votes with respect to any securities of the other Party or with respect to any plan of reorganization filed by the other Party or any other person in connection with a bankruptcy or similar proceeding under state or federal law involving the other Party or any of its subsidiaries, (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing or (iv) make any public announcement with respect to the foregoing. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement (including the foregoing), nothing in this Section 4(d) shall prohibit either Party from submitting a confidential proposal to the other Party’s Board of Directors (or similar governing body) with respect to any action described in this Section 4(d)(I), provided that such proposal is not of a type that would require the other Party to make a public disclosure thereof. This restriction will automatically be lifted if either party receives an unsolicited offer from a third party during this period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the sentence: 'This restriction will automatically be lifted if either party receives an unsolicited offer from a third party during this period.' This directly contradicts the earlier part of the same section which states that 'Each Party is furnishing its Information hereunder in consideration of the other Party’s agreement for a period of eighteen (18) months'. The added sentence allows for the restrictions to be lifted before the eighteen-month period is over, creating an inconsistency as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date hereof, neither COMPANY nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of XPLORE, (ii) management-level employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date hereof, neither XPLORE nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of COMPANY, (ii) management-level employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement will not preclude COMPANY, XPLORE or any of their respective subsidiaries from (A) hiring any employee of the type described in clause (iii) above of the other Party who responds to an advertisement or general solicitation (including through recruiting firms or similar engagements) that is not specifically targeted at such employee or at employees of such other Party generally or (B) making any such general solicitation.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date hereof, neither COMPANY nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of XPLORE, (ii) management-level employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. For a period of twelve (12) months from the date hereof, neither XPLORE nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of COMPANY, (ii) management-level employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement will not preclude COMPANY, XPLORE or any of their respective subsidiaries from (A) hiring any employee of the type described in clause (iii) above of the other Party who responds to an advertisement or general solicitation (including through recruiting firms or similar engagements) that is not specifically targeted at such employee or at employees of such other Party generally or (B) making any such general solicitation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the restriction period from eighteen (18) months to twelve (12) months for XPLORE creates an in-text contradiction. The first part of the paragraph restricts COMPANY from soliciting employees of XPLORE for 18 months, while the second part restricts XPLORE from soliciting employees of COMPANY for 12 months. This difference in duration creates uncertainty and is contradictory regarding the actual length of the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date hereof, neither COMPANY nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of XPLORE, (ii) management-level employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of XPLORE or its affiliates with whom COMPANY or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. For a period of twelve (12) months from the date hereof, neither XPLORE nor its subsidiaries will, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire or hire any (i) officers of COMPANY, (ii) management-level employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives have had direct contact in connection with the evaluation or negotiation of a possible transaction, or (iii) employees of COMPANY or its affiliates with whom XPLORE or its Representatives is first made aware of in connection with any due diligence conducted in connection with the evaluation of a possible transaction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement will not preclude COMPANY, XPLORE or any of their respective subsidiaries from (A) hiring any employee of the type described in clause (iii) above of the other Party who responds to an advertisement or general solicitation (including through recruiting firms or similar engagements) that is not specifically targeted at such employee or at employees of such other Party generally or (B) making any such general solicitation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-solicitation period for XPLORE from eighteen (18) months to twelve (12) months, while the non-solicitation period for COMPANY remains at eighteen (18) months. This creates an inconsistency and direct conflict within the same paragraph of the contract, specifically paragraph 5, as the two parties are now bound by different non-solicitation durations. The contradicted text is the same location as the changed text, meaning that the changed duration causes the contradiction by differing from the original duration.

perturbation.original_text: 6. For purposes of complying with the obligations set forth herein, each of COMPANY and XPLORE shall use efforts fully commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its privileged and confidential Information. Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party. Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information. All intellectual property rights relating to any drawings, documents and work carried out by any Party (whether past, present or future) using the other Party’s Information will belong to and will vest in the other Party. Each Party agrees that it will do all such things and execute all documents necessary to enable the other Party to obtain, defend or enforce its rights in such drawings, documents and work.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. For purposes of complying with the obligations set forth herein, each of COMPANY and XPLORE shall use efforts fully commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its privileged and confidential Information, however, such efforts are only required during normal business hours. Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party. Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information. All intellectual property rights relating to any drawings, documents and work carried out by any Party (whether past, present or future) using the other Party’s Information will belong to and will vest in the other Party. Each Party agrees that it will do all such things and execute all documents necessary to enable the other Party to obtain, defend or enforce its rights in such drawings, documents and work.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'however, such efforts are only required during normal business hours' to the first sentence of section 6, the level of effort required for protecting confidential information is being changed and it is creating an in-text contradiction. The original language suggests a continuous obligation to protect the information. The change now limits that obligation to normal business hours, creating a conflict about the scope of protection and potential liability for breaches occurring outside those hours.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. For purposes of complying with the obligations set forth herein, each of COMPANY and XPLORE shall use efforts fully commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its privileged and confidential Information, however, such efforts are only required during normal business hours. Each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries or affiliates has been granted any license, copyright or other similar right or privilege with respect to any of the Information or other information provided by or on behalf of the other Party. Each Party hereby acknowledges and confirms that all existing and future intellectual property rights relating to the other Party’s Information are the exclusive property of such Party. Each Party agrees that it will not apply for or obtain any intellectual property protection in respect of the other Party’s Information. All intellectual property rights relating to any drawings, documents and work carried out by any Party (whether past, present or future) using the other Party’s Information will belong to and will vest in the other Party. Each Party agrees that it will do all such things and execute all documents necessary to enable the other Party to obtain, defend or enforce its rights in such drawings, documents and work.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 6 to limit the obligation to protect confidential information to 'normal business hours'. This directly contradicts the implicit continuous obligation to protect the information present in the unchanged portions of the same section, as well as the stated purpose of using efforts 'fully commensurate' with protection of its own information. The added phrase creates a conflict and an inconsistency within the document regarding the scope of protection, which creates an in-text contradiction. Based on the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: 9. It is understood that this Agreement does not obligate COMPANY or XPLORE to enter into any further agreement. Unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and XPLORE with respect to a transaction has been executed and delivered, neither COMPANY nor XPLORE will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any written or oral expression with respect to any transaction by any of the COMPANY’s or XPLORE’s Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each Party understands and agrees that (i) the other Party (a) shall be free to conduct the process for a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such process, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or entering into a definitive agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each case, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall be free at its sole discretion to at any time accept or reject any proposal relating to the other Party for any reason without notice and (ii) it shall have no claim against the other Party or any of its officers, directors, employees, shareholders, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants or other agents or representatives in connection with any of the foregoing matters.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. It is understood that this Agreement does not obligate COMPANY or XPLORE to enter into any further agreement, unless both parties have already began to evaluate assets or businesses. Unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and XPLORE with respect to a transaction has been executed and delivered, neither COMPANY nor XPLORE will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any written or oral expression with respect to any transaction by any of the COMPANY’s or XPLORE’s Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each Party understands and agrees that (i) the other Party (a) shall be free to conduct the process for a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such process, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or entering into a definitive agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each case, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall be free at its sole discretion to at any time accept or reject any proposal relating to the other Party for any reason without notice and (ii) it shall have no claim against the other Party or any of its officers, directors, employees, shareholders, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants or other agents or representatives in connection with any of the foregoing matters.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion 'unless both parties have already began to evaluate assets or businesses' into section 9 contradicts the following statement that 'this Agreement does not obligate COMPANY or XPLORE to enter into any further agreement.' The original text ensures that neither party is forced into a future agreement, but the added caveat now suggests a conditional obligation based on preliminary evaluations, thus creating a direct in-text contradiction. The insertion creates doubt as to whether there truly is no future agreement to be entered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. It is understood that this Agreement does not obligate COMPANY or XPLORE to enter into any further agreement, unless both parties have already began to evaluate assets or businesses. Unless and until a definitive agreement between COMPANY and XPLORE with respect to a transaction has been executed and delivered, neither COMPANY nor XPLORE will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any written or oral expression with respect to any transaction by any of the COMPANY’s or XPLORE’s Representatives except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each Party understands and agrees that (i) the other Party (a) shall be free to conduct the process for a Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine (including changing or terminating such process, providing any information to any other Person, negotiating with any other Person or entering into a definitive agreement with any other Person with respect to any transaction, in each case, at any time and without notice to you or any other Person) and (b) shall be free at its sole discretion to at any time accept or reject any proposal relating to the other Party for any reason without notice and (ii) it shall have no claim against the other Party or any of its officers, directors, employees, shareholders, partners, members, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants or other agents or representatives in connection with any of the foregoing matters.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 9 adds the phrase 'unless both parties have already began to evaluate assets or businesses,' which creates a conditional obligation for the parties to potentially enter into a further agreement. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the same section, 'this Agreement does not obligate COMPANY or XPLORE to enter into any further agreement,' because it now suggests that such an obligation could exist under specific circumstances. This is a clear in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' and the Perturbation Explanation. Since the changed text directly contradicts a statement within the contradicted text, it alters the meaning and enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 12. (a) Upon the request of COMPANY, XPLORE will return or destroy (at XPLORE’S option) all COMPANY Information and any notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information, including all copies thereof, then in the possession of XPLORE or its Representatives. Such return, however, does not abrogate the continuing obligations of XPLORE under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house or external attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process. Any destruction will be certified by an officer of XPLORE, and all retained information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. (a) Upon the request of COMPANY, XPLORE will return or destroy (at XPLORE’S option) all COMPANY Information and any notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information, including all copies thereof, then in the possession of XPLORE or its Representatives. Such return, however, does not abrogate the continuing obligations of XPLORE under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure, but must be destroyed within one year, and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process. Any destruction will be certified by an officer of XPLORE, and all retained information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'but must be destroyed within one year' which was added, creates an in-text contradiction. The original paragraph allows XPLORE's in-house or external attorneys to retain a copy of the COMPANY Information indefinitely. The added phrase now imposes a one-year limit for the in-house attorneys copies, creating a conflict about the permissible retention period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. (a) Upon the request of COMPANY, XPLORE will return or destroy (at XPLORE’S option) all COMPANY Information and any notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information, including all copies thereof, then in the possession of XPLORE or its Representatives. Such return, however, does not abrogate the continuing obligations of XPLORE under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one copy of the COMPANY Information and the notes, correspondence, analyses, documents or other records containing COMPANY Information may be retained by XPLORE’s in-house attorneys to prevent possible future misunderstandings regarding the scope of the disclosure, but must be destroyed within one year, and XPLORE will not be required to destroy electronic back-up versions of the COMPANY Information to the extent such destruction is not reasonably practical; provided that any COMPANY Information retained on routine computer system back-up tapes, disks or other back-up storage devices shall not be used, disclosed or otherwise recovered from such back-up devices unless required for regulatory purposes or legal process. Any destruction will be certified by an officer of XPLORE, and all retained information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'but must be destroyed within one year' to the clause regarding retention of COMPANY Information by XPLORE's in-house attorneys. This directly contradicts the original text which allowed for indefinite retention of the information. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The explanation given states that this change creates a conflict about the permissible retention period, because originally, there was no time limit.

perturbation.original_text: 13. The obligation of each of COMPANY and XPLORE to comply with the provisions contained herein shall continue for a period of four(4) years commencing upon the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. The obligation of each of COMPANY and XPLORE to comply with the provisions contained herein shall continue for a period of four(4) years commencing upon the date hereof, unless superceded by a new directive from the board of directors.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'unless superceded by a new directive from the board of directors' to section 13, there is now an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the obligations. The clause allows for the board to issue a new directive which conflicts with the 4 year restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. The obligation of each of COMPANY and XPLORE to comply with the provisions contained herein shall continue for a period of four(4) years commencing upon the date hereof, unless superceded by a new directive from the board of directors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the board of directors to supersede the initial four-year obligation. This directly contradicts the original intent of the obligation lasting for a defined period, as the board can now alter this duration. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and it reinforces the contradiction because that text is supposed to be a fixed term.
